message:
fix: UX gaps in demo mode, agent messaging, Helm pre-flight (#6338-#6342) (#6345)

Closes #6338: Auto-login on the hosted demo domain (previously only Netlify
preview/netlify.app hostnames triggered auto-login, so users hitting
console.kubestellar.io directly saw a dead-end GitHub OAuth button). Also
adds an explicit "Hosted demo" notice above the Sign in with GitHub button
and disables the button when on the hosted domain so users understand why
real OAuth is unavailable.

Closes #6339: Agent dropdown message is now context-aware. When demo mode is
forced (hosted demo domain), the dropdown explains "Agent not available in
the hosted demo — self-host the console to connect a local agent" with an
inline self-host link, instead of the generic "Agent connection bypassed in
demo mode" string that was shown in all three (hosted / local-no-agent /
misconfigured) cases.

Closes #6342: Adds deploy/helm/kubestellar-console/templates/NOTES.txt with
a post-install pre-flight Secret validation checklist. When any *.existingSecret
is set, prints the exact `kubectl get secret` command the user should run to
verify the referenced Secret exists, plus a troubleshoot command for the
resulting CreateContainerConfigError. Helm can't query the cluster at template
time, so NOTES.txt is the right place for this guidance.

Closes #6340: Already handled — FeatureRequestModal polls
/api/github/token/status on open, disables the Submit button, and renders an
inline "GitHub integration not configured" warning with a link to Console
Settings. No code changes needed; included here to close the issue with the
rest of the batch.

Closes #6341: Already handled — Layout.tsx mounts a "Backend connection lost"
banner driven by the existing useBackendHealth hook (15s poll, 4-failure
threshold), plus a separate offline banner when the agent is also down. The
existing implementation already covers the port-forward-drops case described
in the issue. No code changes needed; included here to close the issue with
the rest of the batch.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -283,7 +283,11 @@ export function AgentStatusIndicator() { })()}

{isDemoMode - ? t('agent.agentBypassedInDemo') + ? isDemoModeForced + // Hosted demo (e.g. console.kubestellar.io): agent can never connect + // from this origin, so point the user at the self-host path. + ? t('agent.hostedDemoBypassed') + : t('agent.agentBypassedInDemo') : isDegraded ? t('agent.connectedButErrors', { count: dataErrorCount }) : isConnected @@ -293,6 +297,20 @@ export function AgentStatusIndicator() { : t('agent.unableToConnect') }

+ {/* When running on the hosted demo, surface a self-host link so + users who want real cluster data know where to go next. */} + {isDemoMode && isDemoModeForced && ( +

+ + {t('agent.selfHostToConnect')} + +

+ )} {!isDemoMode && isDegraded && lastDataError && (

{t('agent.lastError', { error: lastDataError })}

@@ -1549,6 +1549,8 @@
     "clusterMode": "Cluster Mode",
     "localAgentDisconnectedLabel": "Local Agent: Disconnected",
     "agentBypassedInDemo": "Agent connection bypassed in demo mode",
+    "hostedDemoBypassed": "Agent not available in the hosted demo — self-host the console to connect a local agent and view real cluster data",
+    "selfHostToConnect": "Self-host the console to connect a cluster →",
     "connectedButErrors": "Connected but experiencing data errors ({{count}} in last minute)",
     "connectedButErrors_one": "Connected but experiencing data errors ({{count}} in last minute)",
     "connectedButErrors_other": "Connected but experiencing data errors ({{count}} in last minute)",
